actionable_feedback: Attach the executed notebook outputs: include the executed .ipynb with outputs or an nbconvert HTML snapshot so graders/verifiers can inspect cell outputs directly.

Add an explicit per-record economic_impact_score column in optimizer_note.md and the notebook; compute it per USD (objective_value normalized by shot_noise_sd and by decision_budget_usd) or, if decision_budget_usd is not present in the packet, add a short 'economic assumptions' section that defines the normalization policy and shows sample arithmetic for each record.

Insert a small unit-test cell in the notebook that programmatically asserts the reported classifications (supported/needs_review) and reproduces the +/-5% sensitivity outcomes (baseline, +5%, -5%) for each record; save the test result in the executed notebook.
Add a per-record perturbation table (baseline, +5%, -5%) into both the notebook and optimizer_note.md so sensitivity computations can be audited line-by-line.

Add reproducibility metadata: Python major/minor version, critical package versions (or a requirements.txt), a seed policy (if any), and exact commands to rerun the notebook (nbconvert or jupyter nbconvert --execute).

Make the record-to-packet-field citations explicit in the recommendation table (e.g., 'packet.fields: record_id->field_name') instead of numeric tuples, so each reported value can be traced back to the input packet.

Resolve the metadata inconsistencies flagged by the verifier: ensure the grader/verifier summary fields (passed_criteria, failed_criteria, artifact_content_preview_truncated) consistently reflect the rubric item scores and preview state. If the inconsistency originates from the grader/metadata generator, add a short metadata note documenting the discrepancy and the authoritative rubric item scores.

feedback_summary: Solid baseline: both the human-readable optimizer_note.md and the qaoa_landscape_review.ipynb reproduce the record-level classifications and sensitivity logic (supported: R1,R4; needs_review: R2,R3). Notebook was initially fixed and revalidated. Main gaps are (1) missing explicit per-USD economic metrics / ROI arithmetic tied to decision_budget_usd (or an explicit handling policy if that field is absent), (2) missing executed-notebook outputs (nbconverted HTML or executed ipynb) and a unit-test cell that asserts baseline vs +/-5% perturbation behavior for auditability, and (3) reproducibility metadata (environment, package versions, requirements). There are also metadata inconsistencies surfaced by the verifier (conflicting passed/failed rubric indicators and preview-truncation flags) that should be clarified in the submission metadata.

verifier_issues.summary: Conflicting passed/failed rubric indicators in grader metadata; verify authoritative rubric_item_scores when revising.
Preview truncation flags differ across metadata sections; include full executed notebook artifact to avoid truncation concerns.
